rusty_sword.name: Rusty Sword
rusty_sword.description: Older than you. Still sharp enough.

steel_sword.name: Steel Sword
steel_sword.description: Emberhollow's finest. Which isn't saying much.

shadow_cloak.name: Shadow Cloak
shadow_cloak.description: Grey as doubt. Hems that drink the light.

scholars_ring.name: Scholar's Ring
scholars_ring.description: Engraved with a sentence no one finishes.

torch.name: Torch
torch.description: The barrow is darker than night. You'll want this.

healing_draught.name: Healing Draught
healing_draught.description: Bitter, warm, and worth it. Restores 2d4 health.

herbs.name: Greycap Herbs
herbs.description: Ash-pale mushrooms. The hermit prizes these.

wolf_pelt.name: Wolf Pelt
wolf_pelt.description: Thick winter fur. The blacksmith pays well for these.

warding_charm.name: Warding Charm
warding_charm.description: Bone and silver wire. The dead respect old debts.

barrow_key.name: Barrow Key
barrow_key.description: Black iron, cold in every season.

ashen_crown.name: The Ashen Crown
ashen_crown.description: It is not heavy. That is the worst part.

rest: say You eat, sleep badly, and wake stiff but stronger. Health: [red]{health}/12[/]. Supplies left: {supplies}.
drink_draught: say The draught burns going down, then soothes. Health: [red]{health}/12[/].

do: say Under a rotten log: [green]greycap herbs[/]!
say A traveler's purse, half-buried. [yellow]+{find} gold.[/]
say Burrs, mud, and a very indignant squirrel. Nothing useful.
say A low growl rolls out of the bracken behind you...
say The road is quiet. Too quiet, probably, but quiet.
say A tinker's cart rattles past. She tosses you a free sample with a wink: [green]a healing draught[/].
say Arrows thud into the dirt at your feet. [red]Ambush![/]
say Your copper splashes. Moments later a bucket creaks up on its own, holding [yellow]{find} gold[/]. The well keeps change.
say The well whispers back: [italic]"The dead king's fire went out. His want didn't."[/] You feel slightly wiser.
say Nothing. Somewhere down there, your coin lands on a heap of other people's hopes.

pack_hunter.name: Pack Hunter
pack_hunter.description: Carry three wolf pelts at once.

silver_tongue.name: Silver Tongue
silver_tongue.description: Settle the bandit problem without drawing steel.

high_roller.name: High Roller
high_roller.description: Win the innkeeper's dice game three times.

old_debts.name: Old Debts
old_debts.description: Send the barrow wraith to rest without a fight.

untouchable.name: Untouchable
untouchable.description: Stand before the Crowned King without a scratch.

ash_and_dust.name: Ash and Dust
ash_and_dust.description: Give the crown the ending it was owed.

awakening.title: The Notice
awakening.text:
The notice is nailed to a gallows, which tells you something about Emberhollow's optimism:

[italic]"The old king was burned with his crown, yet his barrow lights at night and his roads empty by day. WANTED: someone expendable. Reward: generous. — The Village Council"[/]

You've been called worse than expendable.
on_first_enter: ask name = What name do you go by, traveler?
say [dim]"Well then, {name}," you mutter, pulling your hood up. "Let's go be expendable."[/]

choices.text: I am a [red]sellsword[/] — problems fear my arm (might 3, cunning 1)
do: say [dim]Steel settles into your grip like an old argument.[/]

choices.text: I am a [cyan]thief[/] — problems never see me (cunning 3, might 1)
do: say [dim]The cloak settles over your shoulders and your outline... blurs.[/]

choices.text: I am a [magenta]scholar[/] — problems are merely unread pages (lore 3, cunning 1)
do: say [dim]The ring hums faintly, like a library holding its breath.[/]

emberhollow.title: Emberhollow
emberhollow.text:
Emberhollow huddles under the Barrowfell hills like a dog that knows it's about to be kicked. Smoke, mud, shuttered windows — and every doorway watching you, the new expendable.

The [cyan]Ashen Flagon[/] inn glows warmest. The [cyan]blacksmith[/] rings steadily. The [cyan]apothecary's[/] window is full of jars you'd rather not name. A beggar sits by the [cyan]old well[/].

do: say He looks up, and for one strange moment his eyes are clear as winter. [italic]"The dead remember kindness, {name}. Sooner than you'd think."[/]

choices.text: Toss a coin down the old well
Take the gates out of town

crossroads.title: The Crossroads
crossroads.text:
Three ways from the leaning signpost:

West, the [green]Greywood[/] — herbs, wolves, and a hermit who talks to neither.
East, the [yellow]Old Road[/] — the merchant route the bandits now tax at arrowpoint.
North, the [magenta]Barrowfell[/] — where the dead king sleeps badly.

{if supplies >= 1}Your pack holds {supplies} supplies — enough to camp here if needed.{else}[dim]Your pack is empty. No camping without supplies.[/]{end}

choices.text: Make camp and rest (1 supply, heals 2d4)

forest_edge.title: The Greywood
forest_edge.text:
Grey trunks, grey moss, grey light. The Greywood doesn't rustle so much as [italic]mutter[/].

Mushroom rings dot the loam, and two paths wander deeper — one toward a thread of chimney smoke, one toward a smell of musk and old bones.
forest_edge.revisit_text: The Greywood mutters on. {if wolf_slain}With the big wolf dead, the underbrush feels almost peaceful.{else}Something big still moves out there, keeping pace with you.{end}

choices.text: Wander deeper along the game trails

clearing.title: The Hermit's Clearing
clearing.text:
A crooked hut on a stump-studded clearing, its chimney smoking in a shade of blue smoke has no business being. Wind chimes made of finger bones ting gently.

By the door, a bed of [green]greycap herbs[/] grows in neat, impossible rows.

choices.text: Pocket some of the hermit's herbs

do: say You palm a handful of greycaps without the chimes so much as tinkling.
else: say The bone chimes SHRIEK. From inside: [italic]"THOSE COST EXTRA, CHILD."[/] You leave the herbs and your dignity.
set renown = renown - 1

wolf_den.title: The Wolf Den
wolf_den.text:
Bones ring a hollow under a fallen oak — deer, sheep, and one boot. A [bold]winter wolf[/] the size of a pony rises from the shadows, breath steaming.

{if equipped('shadow_cloak')}It sniffs, puzzled. Your cloak makes you hard to fix eyes on.{end}
wolf_den.revisit_text: {if wolf_slain}The den is silent now. Smaller wolves have already begun to squabble over the old one's throne.{else}The winter wolf is still here, and still enormous.{end}

choices.text: Fight the winter wolf (your 1d20 + might vs its 1d20 + 3)

do: say Your blade finds the throat mid-leap. The forest exhales.
say You and the wolf circle, feint, and break apart — both breathing hard, neither bleeding. Yet.
else: say Teeth rake your shoulder as you stumble back.
call wound

choices.text: Track the younger wolves for pelts

do: say A clean chase, a cleaner kill. Another pelt for the pile.
else: say The young wolves are faster than their late king. One nips you on the way out.
call wound

old_road.title: The Old Road
old_road.text:
Rutted, weed-cracked, and lined with the polite kind of warning: wheels of burnt carts, arranged where travelers can see them.

Smoke rises ahead — the bandit camp squats in a toll-house they didn't build.
old_road.revisit_text: {if chief_dealt}The Old Road breathes easier. A first brave merchant cart creaks past, and its driver actually waves.{else}The burnt cart-wheels haven't moved. Neither has the smoke ahead.{end}

bandit_ambush.title: Ambush!
bandit_ambush.text: Two road bandits melt out of the ditch, blades drawn, grins missing several teeth between them.

choices.text: Cut your way through (1d20 + might vs 1d20 + 2)

do: say They flee, dropping a purse in the panic. [yellow]+{find} gold.[/]
else: say A cudgel catches your ribs before you drive them back.
call wound

choices.text: Vanish into the treeline (1d20 + cunning vs 12)

do: say You're gone before their grins finish forming.
else: say A thrown knife clips you as you run.
call wound
goto old_road

bandit_camp.title: The Toll-House
bandit_camp.text:
{if chief_dealt}The toll-house stands quiet. {if chief_spared}The bandits are packing to leave, and their chief gives you a grudging, complicated nod.{else}Crows have claimed the palisade. Nobody collects tolls anymore.{end}{else}Firelight, laughter with edges, and a dozen armed silhouettes. On a chair of stacked cargo sits the [bold]Bandit Chief[/], flipping a black iron key across her knuckles.

That would be the [cyan]barrow key[/] the innkeeper mentioned — the one the council lost along with its last three expendables.{end}

choices.text: Walk in and talk to the chief

do: say Under false coins and real cobwebs: [yellow]{find} gold[/] and a healing draught.

bandit_fight.title: Steel in the Toll-House
bandit_fight.text:
Tables overturn. The bandits form a ring — this is between you and the chief now, by whatever passes for honor here.

She draws two long knives and smiles like a bad omen.

choices.text: Fight the chief (your 1d20 + might vs her 1d20 + 5)

do: say Her knives ring off your guard once, twice — then she's down, laughing at the sky. [italic]"Take the key, hero. This road's gone sour anyway."[/]
else: say She fights like rent is due. You reel back, bleeding.
call wound

barrow_gate.title: The Barrowfell Gate
barrow_gate.text:
The hill splits into a doorway of black stone, older than the kingdom that borrowed it. An iron gate seals it, its lock shaped like a screaming mouth.

{if heard_wraith}The innkeeper's warning circles your head: something walks between the gate and the crown, and steel alone won't settle it.{end}

choices.text: Unlock the gate with the barrow key
do: say The key turns. The scream-lock swallows it whole and the gate grinds open.
choices.locked_text: the lock wants its black iron key

choices.text: Pick the ancient lock (1d20 + cunning vs 15)

do: say Old locks have old habits. Yours are worse. [green]Click.[/]
else: say The lock BITES — a needle in the mechanism.
call wound

barrow_dark.title: The Descending Dark
barrow_dark.text:
Stairs corkscrew down into a dark with [bold]texture[/] — it presses on your face like cloth.

{if has('torch')}Your torch pushes it back a grudging armslength.{else}[red]You cannot see your own hands. Going deeper without light would be suicide.[/]{end}

do: say A stair gives way — a scything blade of black iron whips from the wall!
else: say You catch the seam of a false stair just in time, and step long over a scythe-trap dusted with old, brown stains.

choices.locked_text: too dark — you need a torch

barrow_hall.title: The Hall of the Sleepless
barrow_hall.text:
A vaulted hall of standing biers, each with its sleeping knight of dust. Between you and the far door, the air curdles into a shape — a [bold magenta]wraith[/] in funeral finery, its face a smear of grief.

[italic]"The king dreams,"[/] it breathes. [italic]"None may wake him."[/]
barrow_hall.revisit_text: {if wraith_banished or visited('crown_chamber')}The hall stands open, its guardian gone to whatever rest it was owed. The dust knights sleep on.{else}The wraith re-forms between the biers, patient as grief itself.{end}

choices.if: has('warding_charm') and not wraith_banished and not visited('crown_chamber')
do: say You lift the bone-and-silver charm. The wraith stares... then [bold]bows[/], grief unknotting from its face like smoke. [italic]"An old debt, honored. Pass, {name}."[/] It pours itself into the nearest bier and is gone.

choices.text: Fight the wraith (your 1d20 + might vs its 1d20 + 7)

do: say Your blade passes through cold silk and something tears that was never cloth. The wraith unravels with a sound like a sigh.
else: say Its touch is a winter ten years long.
call wound
call wound

choices.text: Pass through to the crown chamber

crown_chamber.title: The Crown Chamber
crown_chamber.text:
The chamber is a bowl of black glass, and at its center, on a throne of fused ash, sits the [bold]dead king[/] — and on his brow, unburnt, unbothered, the [bold magenta]Ashen Crown[/].

{if king_defeated}The king's husk lies still at the throne's foot. The crown waits on the glass, patient as ever.{else}The husk stirs. Joints crack like green wood. He rises, and the crown [bold]brightens[/].{end}
on_first_enter: say [italic]The crown speaks without sound: "EXPENDABLE. YES. THEY ALWAYS SEND ME EXPENDABLES. COME. BE KEPT."[/]

choices.text: Fight the Crowned King (1d20 + might + renown vs his 1d20 + 9)
do: say [dim]Your renown steels you — every good deed a plate of armor. (+{renown} to your roll)[/]
contest crown_fight = 1d20 + might + renown vs crown_king
say The husk shatters like a burnt log kicked apart. The crown rolls free across the glass, ringing, [bold]furious[/].
else: say The dead king's grip is a landslide in slow motion.
call wound
call wound

choices.locked_text: requires the fallen king AND the hermit's rite

choices.text: Take the crown and leave it unworn
do: say It comes free with no resistance at all — which is exactly as reassuring as it sounds.

choices.text: Set the crown upon your own head
Walk away from all of it

ending_ashes.title: The Rite of Ashes
ending_ashes.text:
You speak the hermit's words backward, the way grief runs. The crown screams — sound this time, real sound — and then it is only ash, and the ash is only quiet.

The barrow lights go out. All of them. For good.

{if chief_spared}On the road home, the bandit chief's people fall in beside you like an honor guard, because stories need witnesses.{end}
{if wraith_banished and not king_defeated}{end}Emberhollow doesn't cheer — it's not that kind of town — but doors stand open as you pass, and that is louder.

[bold bright_yellow]{name} the Expendable did not come back. {name} the Crownbreaker did.[/]

[dim]Renown: {renown}   Health: {health}/12   Gold: {gold}[/]

ending_crowned.title: Coronation
ending_crowned.text:
It is not heavy. That is the worst part.

The crown settles, and Emberhollow's lights below the hills look suddenly like [bold]yours[/] — the inn, the forge, the little well, all of it owed to you, all of it [italic]kept[/].

{if renown >= 3}Somewhere under the crown's murmur, the person the village was learning to trust politely stops existing.{else}It's fine. Nobody down there ever met your eye anyway.{end}

The barrow gate grinds shut behind its new king.

[bold magenta]Long live {name} the First. Long live {name} the Only.[/]

ending_flee.title: The Long Road
ending_flee.text:
Some jobs are finished. Some are just [italic]left[/].

You walk out of the Barrowfell with your health, most of your gold, and a story you'll tell wrong on purpose for the rest of your life.

{if has('ashen_crown')}The crown rides in your pack, wrapped in three cloths and a prayer. Some nights it murmurs. You've started sleeping farther from your own bags — and one day, somewhere far from here, you'll have to decide all over again.{else}{if king_defeated}Behind you, a crown sits on black glass beside a broken husk, waiting — patient as ever — for the next expendable.{else}Behind you, the barrow lights burn on, and Emberhollow keeps paying its strange tithes.{end}{end}

[bold]{name} lived. It's not nothing.[/]

[dim]Renown: {renown}   Gold: {gold}[/]

death.title: Expendable
death.text:
The council's notice was honest, at least.

They nail a new one to the gallows within the week — same words, fresh nail — and the Barrowfell lights burn on, patient as debt.

[dim]Here ended {name}, who was owed better. Renown: {renown}.[/]

blacksmith.name: Harrow & Daughter, Smiths
blacksmith.greeting: "Buying, or just warming yourself at my forge? Either way, mind the sparks."

on_buy: say [dim]"Da's best blade," the smith says. "Try not to die holding it — bad for word of mouth."[/]

apothecary.name: The Crooked Jar
apothecary.greeting: "Welcome, welcome. Everything's fresher than it smells."

on_buy: say [dim]The apothecary lowers her voice: "Bone and silver. The dead honor old debts — show it, don't swing it."[/]

hello.text: "{name}, was it? Word's around. Sit — the stew's brown and the beer's wet, which is all I promise." He polishes a mug that will never be clean. "Ask, drink, or gamble. In any order."

responses.text: (lore) "Your inn's sign — the Ashen Flagon. Named for the crown?"

barrow_rumor.text: His polishing hand stops. "Three went up before you. Only sounds came back." He leans in. "Between the gate and the crown something [bold]walks[/], and it isn't a thing you cut. The Crooked Jar keeps... arrangements... for the dead. Ask there, if your purse is braver than your sword."

hermit_rumor.text: "Hermit in the Greywood. Talks to mushrooms, and worse — they answer. If anyone knows how to put burnt kings back in the ground [bold]properly[/], it's her. She trades in greycap herbs, not coin. And not manners."

bandit_rumor.text: "Toll-house crew on the Old Road. Their chief's no butcher — she's a businesswoman with knives. And here's the sour part: she holds the [cyan]barrow key[/] the council lost with its last hire." He shrugs. "Talk or fight. She respects both, in her way."

scholar_talk.text: He blinks — nobody's ever asked. "Aye. Grandda served the old king. Said at the end His Majesty stopped sleeping — stood at windows all night whispering [italic]'mine'[/] at the fields. They burned the crown WITH him to be safe." A dry laugh. "You can see how well that took. Have a drink on the house, scholar — one per lifetime."
do: say [dim]Free ale and a primary source. (+1 lore)[/]

dice_game.text: He rattles a cup of dice older than the floorboards. "Knuckle-and-Bones. Your 2d6 against mine, winner doubles the stake." {if dice_won >= 3}He eyes you sourly. "You again. My dice have started flinching."{end}

dice_wager.text: "Coin on the wood, friend." You have [yellow]{gold} gold[/].
do: ask wager = How much do you wager?
say [green]Your bones roll higher! +{wager} gold — you're at {gold}.[/]
say A dead tie. He slides your stake back with visible relief.
else: say [red]His dice grin up at you. -{wager} gold — you're at {gold}.[/]
say He squints at your offer. "Real coin you actually have, friend. House rule."

door.text: The door opens before you knock. She is small, ancient, and looking at a point two inches behind your eyes. "Expendable," she says, the way others say [italic]'rain again'[/]. "You'll be wanting the Rite of Ashes. They always do, the ones who make it this far."

responses.text: "The Rite of Ashes — what is it?"
(lore) "Let me reason it out from first principles."

rite_what.text: "The crown was forged wanting and burnt wanting, and wanting is all that's left of the man inside it. The Rite gives the want somewhere to [bold]end[/]." She holds out a palm like a dry leaf. "I teach it for greycaps. Or to anyone clever enough to steal it out of my sentences — that's happened twice, and I respected it both times."

rite_herbs.text: She takes the greycaps, sniffs them, and nods once — highest praise. Then she teaches you: seven words said backward, the way grief runs. "Say them over the crown when its wearer is down. Not before. It listens."
do: say [bold magenta]You have learned the Rite of Ashes.[/]

rite_study.text: "Oh, [italic]reason[/] it out?" She grins with more teeth than expected. "Then reason, scholar. What runs backward, ends what cannot end, and is said over kings?"

do: say [bold magenta]"Grief," you answer, "spoken backward." Her grin widens: "Third one ever. The words are these—" You have learned the Rite of Ashes.[/]
else: say "Mm. A prayer? No, child. Come back with greycaps — or a better guess."

approach.text: She doesn't stand. The key keeps walking across her knuckles. "The new expendable. You've got till this key stops moving to interest me, {name}."

responses.text: (cunning) "The council's raising a militia. I'm your warning, not your enemy."
(might) "Give me the key or I take it off whoever's still standing."
"Fifteen gold for the key. Clean and simple."
"I don't talk to road trash." (draw steel)

bluff.text: The key stops. "...A militia." You lay it on thick: numbers, pikes, a councilwoman with a grudge. All invented. Her jaw works. "Sour road anyway," she mutters — and tosses you the key. "We're gone by frost. Tell your militia they're heroes."
do: say [dim]There is no militia. There was never a militia.[/]

threat.text: She looks you over — the arm, the stance, the blade. Bandits are businesspeople; the math is quick. "Hm." A test, then: she nods to her biggest man, who steps in cracking his knuckles. "Convince Bruno, convince me."

do: say Bruno sits down very carefully. The chief laughs, tosses you the key. "Sour road anyway. We're gone by frost."
else: say Bruno convinces YOU, mostly of gravity.
call wound
goto bandit_fight

buy.text: "Fifteen." She weighs the key, then your face, then the key. "Done. Pleasure doing business with the doomed." The gold vanishes into her coat; the key lands cold in your palm.
